title: D'Wopp
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type=Bounty hunter |image=[[File:Dwopp.jpg]] |name=D'Wopp |homeworld=[[Toola/Legends|Toola]]{{StoryCite|story=We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}} |birth= |death=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] (35:3:5 GrS),"[[Empire Blues: The Devaronian's Tale]]" places the [[Death/Legends|death]] of D'Wopp two days after the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Tatooine]], which happened in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] (35:3:3 GrS) according to ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''. [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] |species=[[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height=Over 2.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]In a picture featured in ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', D'Wopp was shown to be taller than [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]]—whose height was established as 2.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''. |mass= |hair=Pale [[Color/Legends|yellow]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Lord D'Wopp's a real prince too. A great hunter of course, even by Whiphid standards.''"
"''He must be looking forward to trying his skill here on Tatooine.''"
"''He's already been scouting the terrains out of the spaceport.|[[Thwim]] and Doikk Na'ts|[[We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale (audio drama)|"We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale" audio drama]]}} [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] '''D'Wopp''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who [[Marriage/Legends|married]] Lady [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]], a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]] and [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] main competitor on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. D'Wopp truly loved to [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]], which ultimately led him to his demise. When he accepted a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] offer for the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] from Jabba during their [[Wedding of Valarian and D'Wopp|wedding]] reception, Valarian [[Death/Legends|killed]] him with her bare, clawed [[Limb/Legends|hands]]. His body was returned to his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Toola/Legends|Toola]], missing more than a few pieces. Only Valarian and [[Lady Valarian's cook|her cook]] knew where the rest went. ==Biography== {{Quote|My mate and I shall celebrate my glorious return. She is Whiphid. She will understand.|D'Wopp, seconds before trying to leave his wedding reception|}} A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]], D'Wopp was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on [[Toola/Legends|Toola]], an [[Water/Legends|ice]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'' located within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} He later joined the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]] profession, which was a somewhat quotidian occupation on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], and he became a [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] of great repute as well as a [[Lord/Legends|lord]]. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooinian]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]] Lady [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]]—a Whiphid herself—chose to procure a mate from her homeworld, and she picked D'Wopp. Valarian had him travel to the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], her base of operations on the planet Tatooine. As soon as he arrived in Mos Eisley, D'Wopp started scouting the surroundings of the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] looking for hunting grounds. Eventually, the [[Wedding of Valarian and D'Wopp|wedding]] ceremony occurred as planned, and everything seemed to go well for the Whiphid hunter, who had found a wealthy and powerful mate who was considered a great beauty by other Whiphids. The couple planned to spend their honeymoon hunting ferocious [[Krayt dragon/Legends|Krayt dragons]], even though it required crossing the territory of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], Valarian's arch-rival on Tatooine. [[File:WeddingOfValarianAndDWopp-SquadronsOverCorellia.jpg|thumb|left|200px|D'Wopp was killed by his own wife during their first domestic dispute.]] After the ceremony, the [[Marriage/Legends|newlyweds]] went to Valarian's ''[[Lucky Despot]]'' [[Casino/Legends|casino]], where a wedding reception was held. The event was attended by many invited guests, and the [[Music/Legends|music]] was supplied by the famous [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] band [[Figrin D'an and the Modal Nodes/Legends|Figrin D'an and the Modal Nodes]]. D'Wopp and Valarian made a grand entrance, with their [[Limb/Legends|arms]] lashed together with a garland of imported [[Plant/Legends|greenery]]. They danced their way to the stage through the assembled guests. When Valarian was asked to check on the kitchen employees, the Whiphid hunter was left alone for a moment. Taking advantage of the Lady's absence, a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Unidentified Duros thug|employee]] of Jabba approached D'Wopp and told him of the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] his master had put on the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] head. At first, the Whiphid hunter told the Duros he did not need [[Currency/Legends|money]], but he experienced a change of [[Heart/Legends|heart]] when he was told Solo was a fierce bait. Eager to prove his worth, D'Wopp decided to leave the reception immediately and investigate Solo's whereabouts. The hunter thought his new wife, a fierce and proud Whiphid like himself, would understand his sudden disappearance. However, the hunter had overestimated the indulgence of his new mate, Valarian. While D'Wopp was preparing to vacate the premises, the Whiphid crime lady reappeared out of the crowd and started insulting him. D'Wopp replied in the same tone, and soon husband and wife started [[Unarmed combat/Legends|attacking each other]] in front of everyone and in the middle of the dance floor. Quickly, the brawl spread among the guests, with Jabba's henchmen cheerleading. In the midst of the altercation, Lady Valarian ended up [[Death/Legends|killing]] her husband with her clawed hands. She was now without a man, and the ''Lucky Despot'' had been wrecked. A few [[Standard day/Legends|days]] later, D'Wopp's body was sent back to Toola in a box, with several parts of his body missing—only the widow and [[Lady Valarian's cook|her cook]] knew what had happened to them.''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Gooood hunting, Whiphid?''"
"''Explain that remark, Duro, or I shall serve your roasted ribs to my lady for breakfast.|A Duro thug and D'Wopp|}} Standing at a height of over 2.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]], D'Wopp was a towering Whiphid with a lumpy head and a coat of pale [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]]. He was known for his ferocity, lending credence to the Whiphid reputation for fierceness.{{DB|character|kkruhk|K'Kruhk}} He was also extremely proud of his well-above average hunting skills, and he would not miss a single opportunity to demonstrate his worth by capturing a "fierce bait." When Jabba's Duros thug approached him about working, D'Wopp was already eager to "splash" a new victim. Although he ultimately abandoned her for hunting on the very day of the wedding reception, Lord D'Wopp was strongly attracted to Valarian—at least on a physical level. The hunter was also quick to take offence when the Duros compared his new bride to a big catch. ==Behind the scenes== Lady Valarian's late husband first appeared in [[August]] [[1995]] in "[[We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale]]," a short story authored by [[Kathy Tyers]] for the anthology ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]''. He was also mentioned in "[[Let Us Prey: The Whiphid's Tale]]," another short story published in [[January]] [[1996]]. In that story, the late hunter was referred to as Valarian's "first husband," suggesting she may have remarried later.{{StoryCite|story=Let Us Prey: The Whiphid's Tale|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}} There is a slight discrepancy in the sources regarding D'Wopp's height. In "The Band's Tale," both D'Wopp and Valarian were described as "two gargantuan Whiphids—two and a half meters of [[Tooth/Legends|tusk]] and claw and pale yellow fur." In the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', D'Wopp was depicted as visibly taller than his bride,''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' whose height was established as 2.5 meters in ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''. However, since the [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Doikk Na'ts/Legends|Doikk Na'ts]] was the [[in-universe]] narrator of "The Band's Tale," his estimate of the Whiphids' respective heights may have been inaccurate. In the [[We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale (audio drama)|audio dramatization]] of "The Band's Tale," D'Wopp was voiced by voice actor/musician [[Danny Louis]] of the band Gov't Mule.{{Facebook|username=dannylouismusician|url=dannylouismusician/posts/1906073576173733?comment_id=1906476476133443|name=Danny Louis|date=October 31, 2018|archivefile=File:Danny_DWopp.png}} His name was {{Pronounced|ˈdu.ˌwɑp}}, which is a homophone of [[Wikipedia:Doo-wop|doo-wop]]—a style of [[Wikipedia:Rhythm and blues|rhythm and blues]] vocalizing developed in the United States in the [[1950s]].{{WebCite|url=http://www.onlinerootsofrock.com/doo-wop|text=Doo-wop|work=The Online Roots of Rock|archivedate=20180818095751}} ==Appearances== *{{SWGTCG|set=Squadrons Over Corellia|scenario=Festival of Love promotional scenario}} *{{StoryCite|story=We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}} {{1st}} *[[We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale (audio drama)|"We Don't Do Weddings: The Band's Tale" audio drama]] *{{StoryCite|story=Empire Blues: The Devaronian's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}} {{Mo}} *{{StoryCite|story=Let Us Prey: The Whiphid's Tale|book=Tales from Jabba's Palace}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{InsiderCite|52|Area Film Satirized—Star Wars in the Onion|Area Film Satirized—''Star Wars'' in the Onion}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{SWGTCG|set=Squadrons Over Corellia|cardname=Scandal}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=D'Wopp |pl=D'Wopp }} [[Category:Bounty hunters]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Whiphids]]
